search_terms_used: long run training adaptations marathon physiology glycogen depletion fat oxidation
long slow distance running physiological adaptations mitochondrial biogenesis endurance
optimal long run distance marathon preparation training volume evidence
Holloszy 1967 biochemical adaptations endurance exercise mitochondrial
coverage_notes: Good coverage of why long runs work: mitochondrial biogenesis (Holloszy foundational, Irrcher review), substrate metabolism (Spriet, Hearris, Impey glycogen threshold), marathon-specific physiology (Coyle, Rapoport), molecular adaptations (Hawley, Hughes), and practical training volume evidence (Fokkema). Limited direct RCTs on long run distance/pace — most evidence is physiological mechanism-based.

papers.title: Biochemical adaptations in muscle. Effects of exercise on mitochondrial oxygen uptake and respiratory enzyme activity in skeletal muscle
papers.notes: Foundational study demonstrating that endurance exercise training doubles mitochondrial oxidative capacity in skeletal muscle (rat model).

papers.title: Regulation of mitochondrial biogenesis in muscle by endurance exercise
papers.notes: Reviewed molecular mechanisms by which endurance exercise regulates mitochondrial biogenesis in skeletal muscle.

papers.title: Physiological regulation of marathon performance
papers.notes: Reviewed the physiological factors regulating marathon performance including aerobic metabolism, carbohydrate stores, and thermoregulation.

papers.title: Metabolic factors limiting performance in marathon runners
papers.notes: Developed a mathematical model of carbohydrate depletion (hitting the wall) during marathon running based on personalized physiological parameters.

papers.title: New insights into the interaction of carbohydrate and fat metabolism during exercise
papers.notes: Reviewed how carbohydrate and fat metabolism interact during exercise and how training alters fuel selection.

papers.title: Maximizing Cellular Adaptation to Endurance Exercise in Skeletal Muscle
papers.notes: Reviewed molecular signaling networks underlying skeletal muscle adaptation to endurance exercise including nutrient-exercise interactions.

papers.title: Regulation of Muscle Glycogen Metabolism during Exercise: Implications for Endurance Performance and Training Adaptations
papers.notes: Reviewed regulation of glycogen metabolism during exercise and implications for endurance performance and training adaptations.

papers.title: Fuel for the Work Required: A Theoretical Framework for Carbohydrate Periodization and the Glycogen Threshold Hypothesis
papers.notes: Proposed the glycogen threshold hypothesis and 'fuel for the work required' framework for carbohydrate periodization in endurance training.

papers.title: Adaptations to Endurance and Strength Training
papers.journal: Cold Spring Harbor Perspectives in Medicine
papers.notes: Reviewed molecular and cellular mechanisms of adaptation to endurance and strength training including mitochondrial biogenesis and angiogenesis.

papers.title: Training for a (half-)marathon: Training volume and longest endurance run related to performance and running injuries
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: Prospective study of 997 marathon/half-marathon runners examining associations between training volume, longest run, performance, and injury.
